mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting present valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 9.0 percent, labor market slack dissipates, triggering wage growth expectations and consumer spending acceleration that boosts corporate earnings and equity valuations within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 9.0, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in real GDP growth and housing starts signals robust aggregate demand expansion, which improves profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in forward 60-day returns during periods where both GDPC1 and HOUST show positive month-over-month changes, or cross-validation IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and supports equity multiples by improving the supply-demand balance for corporate goods.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO rises for three consecutive months, or the signal exhibits a negative rank correlation with SPX over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in real disposable personal income fuels consumer expenditure, directly lifting revenue forecasts for consumer-facing S&P 500 constituents and generating positive forward equity returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in over 60% of observations where RSAFS shows sequential quarterly growth, or predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate above 0.25 percent signals aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity risk premiums and discounts future cash flows more heavily, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly window.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the coefficient on FEDFUNDS in a multivariate regression becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and declining unemployment confirm a self-reinforcing labor market recovery, which reduces uncertainty premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon as earnings guidance improves.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate positive forward returns in more than 45% of months where PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio drops below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts combined with rising real GDP growth indicate peak aggregate demand, which historically precedes inflationary pressures and subsequent monetary tightening, ultimately depressing equity valuations over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are not significantly negative when both HOUST and GDPC1 accelerate simultaneously, or the signal's predictive power reverses sign during periods of CPIAUCSL disinflation.
